milestones.name: Capture + store + view
milestones.outcome: A global docs/shield/backlog.json exists; entries can be added (user + agent) with order, kind, source, and a feature + epic association; /backlog renders the ordered list with per-entry pipeline status from manifest.json; an entry can be manually removed.
exit_criteria: backlog.json has a documented JSON Schema with a top-level schema_version and per-entry {id, order, kind, source, feature, epic, text}; ids are unique across entries[]; shield/scripts/validate_backlog.py exits 0 on valid and non-zero with a named error on invalid.
An entry can be captured both from the user (/backlog add) and from a Shield skill via the documented write helper; the write is atomic (temp-then-rename) and validate-or-refuse.
/backlog renders entries in order with each entry's feature + epic and a research/prd/plan status read from manifest.json.
/backlog can remove an entry by id (plain delete; no retained history).

milestones.name: Feature + epic association + suggestion
milestones.outcome: Every entry references a feature and an epic (existing or proposed-new); the agent suggests a matching feature/epic by scanning manifest.json features and plan.json epics, and the user can accept, pick another, or create-new.
exit_criteria: Capture prompts for (or accepts) a feature + epic; both may be proposed-new.
The agent proposes >=1 candidate feature (from manifest.json) and >=1 candidate epic (from the feature's plan.json) using exact-normalized match; the user can accept/replace/create-new.
Suggestion never blocks capture — an entry can be captured with a proposed-new feature/epic when no match exists; a normalized-name tie surfaces all tied candidates and auto-picks none.

milestones.name: Promotion + reconciliation
milestones.outcome: The user promotes an entry by starting /research, /prd, /plan, or /implement from it; the entry is removed when its work commits — eagerly at the end of the promoted /plan or /implement run, lazily on the /backlog sweep, or manually. Reconciliation matches by feature (manifest) + epic (plan.json) and never removes on doubt.
exit_criteria: Promoting an entry passes it as a transient reference to /plan or /implement; on success that entry is pruned (eager).
The /backlog sweep removes any entry whose epic's work now appears in the feature's plan.json (lazy safety net); a prd-only feature is NOT removed.
Match key: both existing and proposed-new entries match by casefold+collapsed-whitespace exact epic NAME (never by positional epic id); on ambiguity or no match the entry stays.

Eager prune and lazy sweep are idempotent (remove-if-present), share one reconciliation engine, log every removal with rationale, and treat an unrecognized manifest.json/plan.json shape as doubt (entry stays), never crashing.
A .shield.json kill switch (backlog.auto_reconcile=false), made schema-valid by an additive 'backlog' object in shield.schema.json, disables eager prune and lazy sweep, leaving manual-remove only.

An executable eval exercises capture (user + skill), view+status, manual remove, eager prune, lazy sweep, match-key, never-remove-on-doubt, concurrency (no lost entry), no-stamping (F6), and recovery-rehearsal with a RED->GREEN trail; the Shield plugin version is bumped per CLAUDE.md.

stories.name: Define backlog.json schema and validator
stories.description: Define the docs/shield/backlog.json document shape and a machine-readable JSON Schema with a top-level schema_version, plus a Python validator. Each entry carries id, order (integer), kind (epic|story|task), source (user|agent), feature, epic, and text. schema_version is set now so the open future-scope shape changes (priority buckets, audit trail) can migrate via read-old/write-new.
tasks: Author shield/schema/backlog.schema.json (draft 2020-12) with top-level schema_version (integer) and an entries[] array; entry required fields: id, order, kind, source, feature, epic, text.

Specify the id contract: id is a string generated as uuid4 (chosen for collision-free capture with no read-before-write). Remove / promote / prune all key off id. NOTE (plan-review 2026-05-29, P1-2): uniqueness of id across entries[] is enforced by the Python validator, NOT by the JSON Schema — draft 2020-12 uniqueItems is whole-item equality and cannot express property-level uniqueness.

Document the entry shape, the id contract, the schema_version migration policy (read-old/write-new), AND the invariant 'manifest features[].name == feature folder slug' (the reconciliation key, relied on by suggestion + reconciliation — P1-3) in a new shield/skills/general/backlog/SKILL.md (or backlog-schema.md).

Create shield/scripts/validate_backlog.py (pydantic + jsonschema, run via uv) — exits 0 on valid, non-zero with a named error (unknown_kind_enum, missing_required_field, duplicate_entry_id, schema_version_too_new) on invalid. The validator (not the schema) owns the duplicate_entry_id check.
Define ordering as a single explicit integer order field (no priority buckets in v1).
Migration is doc-only until schema_version 2: word the migration AC as 'migration policy documented' (no live migrate() code shipped in v1) to avoid mistaking documentation for working code.
acceptance_criteria: shield/schema/backlog.schema.json validates a well-formed backlog.json and rejects one with an unknown kind value, naming the error.
The validator (validate_backlog.py) rejects an entries[] array containing duplicate id values, naming the error (duplicate_entry_id) — this check lives in the validator, not the JSON Schema.
uv run shield/scripts/validate_backlog.py <path> exits 0 on valid sidecars and non-zero with a named error on invalid ones.

backlog.json carries a top-level schema_version; a documented migration policy (read-old/write-new, doc-only until schema_version 2) and the manifest-name==folder-slug invariant are present in the SKILL.md.
kind is constrained to {epic, story, task}; source to {user, agent}; order is an integer; id is a uuid4 string.

stories.name: Capture entrypoint (user + skill) with atomic write
stories.description: Provide a capture path usable both by the user (/backlog add) and by any Shield skill (a documented write helper). Writes are atomic (temp-then-rename), validate-or-refuse, and carry a compare-before-replace check so a concurrent on-disk change cannot silently drop an entry. This resolves the PRD-review P1 'capture interface undefined' and closes TRD §12 Q3.
tasks: Add a /backlog add path to the backlog command that appends an entry (assigns the next order and a uuid4 id).

LOCKED (plan-review 2026-05-27): write-helper signature capture(text: str, *, kind: str = 'task', feature: str | None = None, epic: str | None = None, source: str) -> str (returns entry id) in shield/scripts/backlog_store.py, raising BacklogInvalid on a malformed/partial store. Pinned in TRD §11. Every capturing skill builds against this.

LOCKED (plan-review 2026-05-27): concurrency = single-writer assumption (no lock); build full doc -> .tmp -> os.replace(); reads validate-or-refuse (TRD §6 N1). Revisit with a lockfile only if Shield becomes multi-actor.

ADDED (plan-review 2026-05-29, P1-1/security P1-a): compare-before-replace — capture()/remove() capture the on-disk schema_version+entry-count (or mtime/hash) at read time and refuse the os.replace() if the file changed underneath, raising BacklogInvalid. Converts a silent lost-update (the real N1/N5 threat) into a loud refusal WITHOUT a lockfile.

ADDED (plan-review 2026-05-29, P1-4): package backlog_store as an importable module with a pyproject.toml (F3 requires skills to import capture()); document the import path. This makes the EPIC-4-S2 version bump unconditional.

Validate-or-refuse on read AND write: a malformed/partial backlog.json never silently truncates and is refused with a named error; a capture() that would produce a schema-invalid in-memory doc refuses before os.replace().
Expose the write helper as the documented contract (fields a skill must pass — text, kind, feature?, epic?, source) so an agent can capture mid-task.
acceptance_criteria: A user can capture an entry via /backlog add; a skill can capture via the documented write helper with source=agent.
The capture interface (command + capture() helper signature/fields) is documented in the backlog SKILL.md and pinned in TRD §11.
Killing capture mid-write leaves no corrupted backlog.json (only a .tmp may remain, cleaned up next run).
A concurrent on-disk change between read and os.replace() is refused with BacklogInvalid (compare-before-replace) — no entry is silently lost.
A new entry is assigned the next integer order, a uuid4 id, and a default kind when unspecified.
A malformed/partial backlog.json on read is refused with a named error (BacklogInvalid), never silently read or truncated.

stories.name: /backlog view — ordered list
stories.description: Implement the /backlog command + skill that renders the current backlog ordered by the order field, showing each entry's feature + epic association and source. Pipeline status badges are added in EPIC-2-S1.
tasks: Author shield/commands/backlog.md and shield/skills/general/backlog/SKILL.md.
Render entries sorted by order, each line showing id, feature, epic, source, and text.
Define the per-entry render line format once in the backlog SKILL.md (the canonical badge string is specified in EPIC-2-S1) so every view path renders identically.
Document a local-dev / dry-run loop in the backlog SKILL.md (how to view against a fixture backlog.json without mutating the project store).
Handle the empty-backlog case with a clear message.
acceptance_criteria: /backlog lists entries in ascending order with feature + epic + source per entry.
An empty backlog renders a clear 'no entries' message, not an error.
The command is registered and discoverable in the Shield plugin.
The render line format is documented once in the backlog SKILL.md and reused by every view path.

stories.name: Manual remove from /backlog
stories.description: Allow explicitly removing an entry from /backlog by id — for ideas decided against and for entries not cleared by a promotion run. Removal is a plain delete (no retained history in v1).
tasks: Add a /backlog remove <id> path that deletes the entry via the atomic write helper.
Confirm-before-delete for a user-initiated remove; surface a clear message if the id is absent.
Document the recoverability boundary: git revert (N4) recovers only entries that reached a commit; a manual remove of an uncommitted entry is unrecoverable by design.
acceptance_criteria: /backlog remove <id> deletes the entry and the change is persisted atomically.
Removing a non-existent id reports a clear error and makes no change.
No history is retained (plain delete), per the v1 decision; the uncommitted-entry recoverability caveat is documented.

stories.name: Per-entry pipeline status from manifest.json
stories.description: Enrich the /backlog view so each entry shows its feature's pipeline status (research / prd / plan) read live from manifest.json artifact flags. This lets an entry show 'prd done, not yet planned' without being removed.
tasks: Read manifest.json; for each entry, look up its feature and surface the artifacts.{research,prd,plan_json} flags as status badges.
Pin the canonical badge string in the backlog SKILL.md: 'research ✓  prd ✓  plan –' (✓ present, – absent); 'not started' when the feature is absent from manifest.json.
Compute status at view time (no stored status field).
acceptance_criteria: /backlog shows research/prd/plan badges per entry derived from manifest.json using the pinned badge string.
An entry whose feature has a prd but no plan shows 'prd ✓ plan –' and remains in the backlog.
An entry whose feature is absent from manifest.json shows 'not started'.

stories.name: Feature + epic association + agent suggestion
stories.description: At capture/promotion, associate every entry with a feature (the reconciliation key) and an epic (the removal gate) — either may be proposed-new. The agent suggests a matching feature (from manifest.json) and epic (from that feature's plan.json), and the user can accept, pick another, or create-new.
tasks: On capture, prompt for / accept a feature + epic; allow proposed-new for either.

LOCKED (plan-review 2026-05-27): match = exact normalized (casefold + collapsed whitespace); no fuzzy/token-overlap ranking in v1. UPDATED (plan-review 2026-05-29, P0-2): both existing AND proposed-new epics match by exact normalized NAME (epic id EPIC-N is a positional within-plan slot, not a cross-plan identity). Specified in TRD §5 F7/F8.

Implement suggestion against the pinned real shapes (P0-1): suggest_feature(text, *, manifest) reads manifest.features[].name; suggest_epic(text, *, feature, plans) reads plans[feature].epics[], where plans is dict[feature-slug -> parsed plan.json] with the path derived as docs/shield/<slug>/plan.json for features whose artifacts.plan_json == true.
suggest_feature returns features[].name, which IS the folder slug (invariant pinned in EPIC-1-S1, P1-3).
Let the user accept the suggestion, choose another, or create-new; never block capture if no match exists; a tie (>=2 normalized matches) surfaces all tied candidates and auto-picks none.
acceptance_criteria: Every captured entry carries a feature + epic association (existing or proposed-new).
The agent proposes >=1 feature and >=1 epic candidate when matches exist; the user can accept/replace/create-new.
Given a fixture manifest with feature 'auth', capturing text mentioning 'auth' surfaces 'auth' as the top candidate; a 2-way normalized-name tie surfaces both and auto-picks neither.
A suggested feature value resolves to an existing docs/shield/<value>/ path (name == folder slug).
Capture still succeeds with a proposed-new feature/epic when no match exists.

stories.name: User-driven promotion with transient reference
stories.description: Let the user promote an entry by starting the Shield step they choose (/research, /prd, /plan, or /implement) from it. The promoted command carries the entry id as a transient runtime reference (NOT an id stamped into plan.json), used to prune the entry on success.
tasks: Add a /backlog promote <id> affordance that launches the user-chosen step and passes the entry id as a transient promotion reference.
Document that the reference is a runtime command argument, never written into plan.json/story records.
Shippable work routes through /plan; direct /implement stays available for rare planless one-offs.
acceptance_criteria: Promoting an entry starts the user-chosen step and forwards the entry id as a transient reference.
The reference is not persisted into plan.json or any story record (no id stamping).
The user chooses the step; the tool never auto-routes.

stories.name: Reconciliation engine (match key + never-remove-on-doubt)
stories.description: Implement the core reconciliation: locate an entry's feature in manifest.json, and if it has a plan.json, check whether the entry's epic landed. The 'epic landed' gate is one precise predicate, used everywhere: an entry is removed when an epic with the matching normalized-exact NAME is present in plan.json.epics[]; story status is NOT consulted. On ambiguity or no match, the entry stays. Treat unrecognized manifest.json/plan.json shapes as doubt (entry stays), never crashing.
tasks: Implement shield/scripts/reconcile_backlog.py with reconcile(entry, *, manifest: dict, plans: dict[str, dict]) -> RemovalDecision as a pure function over already-read docs (P0-1): manifest is the parsed {schema_version, features:[{name, artifacts:{plan_json: bool, ...}}]}; plans maps feature-slug -> parsed plan.json (path derived as docs/shield/<slug>/plan.json, NOT stored in manifest).

UPDATED (plan-review 2026-05-29, P0-2): match key = epic by casefold+collapsed-ws exact NAME for BOTH existing and proposed-new entries. Epic id (EPIC-N) is a positional within-plan slot reassigned on every re-/plan, so it is NOT a cross-plan key; never gate removal on epic id. Tie (>=2 name matches) or no match -> entry stays. Story status is never consulted.
Never remove on ambiguity/no-match; a prd-only feature is not removed.
Read-contract drift tolerance: an unrecognized manifest.json/plan.json shape is treated as doubt (entry stays), with a logged warning — no crash.

Define the RemovalDecision dataclass (REMOVE / STAY_AMBIGUOUS / STAY_NO_MATCH / STAY_DOUBT) carrying the F9 log fields. Log every removal with rationale: {entry id, feature, epic, match-kind (name), triggering run, gating plan.json path} — a confident removal must not be a silent git diff.
acceptance_criteria: An entry is removed only when an epic with normalized-exact NAME is present in plan.json.epics[]; story status is NOT consulted; a prd-only feature is not removed.
Both existing and proposed-new entries match by normalized-exact epic NAME; removal is never gated on epic id; ambiguous/no-match entries stay.
An epic reordered or renumbered across a re-/plan (same name, different EPIC-N) still resolves correctly (name-based, not id-based).
An epic-name collision across two different features is ambiguous → the entry stays (the one place a wrong removal is plausible; PRD §10 risk / §14 trigger).
A malformed or older-shape manifest.json/plan.json results in 'entry stays' (logged), never an exception.
Every removal emits a structured log line carrying {entry id, feature, epic, match-kind, triggering run, gating plan.json path}.

stories.name: Eager + lazy removal triggers (idempotent) + kill switch
stories.description: Wire the two automatic removal triggers on top of the reconciliation engine: eager prune at the end of a promoted /plan or /implement run (using the transient reference), and a lazy sweep on /backlog view. Both are idempotent (remove-if-present) so they can't double-remove or race. Depends on EPIC-3-S1 (transient reference) and EPIC-3-S2 (engine) and must land after both. Ships the kill switch and closes the uncommitted-state recovery gap.
tasks: At the end of /plan and /implement, if the run carried a promotion reference, prune that entry on success (eager).
On /backlog view, run the reconciliation sweep over all entries (lazy safety net).
Make both paths idempotent: removing an already-absent entry is a no-op; ensure the sweep and eager prune share the EPIC-3-S2 reconciliation engine (no divergent logic).

Ship the kill switch: a .shield.json flag backlog.auto_reconcile (default true) that, when false, disables eager prune and lazy sweep, leaving manual-remove only — the §14 rollback fallback. ADDED (plan-review 2026-05-29, P0-3): extend shield/schemas/shield.schema.json with an optional 'backlog' object ({auto_reconcile: bool, default true}) — the current schema has additionalProperties:false, so the flag fails validation without this. Add a config example.

RESOLVED (plan-review 2026-05-29, P1-1): the uncommitted-state recovery mechanism for v1 is the transient append-only .shield/backlog-removed.log — append the pruned entry BEFORE the destructive remove; replaying the log restores it. Commit-before-prune is an explicit non-goal (avoids forcing a possibly-dirty-tree commit on every prune).
A no-op eager prune (entry already removed by the lazy sweep) emits no log line and writes no recovery record.
Instrument the N2 ~1s budget: add a debug-gated latency line to /backlog view that logs WARN if view+sweep > 1s so 'revisit if breached' is falsifiable.
acceptance_criteria: Promoting via /plan or /implement removes the referenced entry at end of run (eager), before the next /backlog view.
The /backlog sweep removes any entry whose work has since landed in a plan.json (lazy).
Running eager prune then the sweep on the same entry is a no-op the second time (idempotent); eager and lazy paths call the same reconciliation engine.
Setting backlog.auto_reconcile=false in .shield.json (now schema-valid) disables both eager prune and lazy sweep, leaving manual-remove functional.
An end-of-run prune appends the entry to .shield/backlog-removed.log before the destructive remove; replaying the log restores the entry (single recovery mechanism).
A debug-gated latency line on /backlog view reports view+sweep wall time and logs WARN above the 1s budget.

stories.name: Executable evals for the backlog lifecycle (RED→GREEN)
stories.description: Per CLAUDE.md's mandatory eval-coverage policy, author executable evals that exercise the new backlog behavior: capture (user + skill), view + pipeline status, manual remove, eager prune, lazy sweep, match-key, never-remove-on-doubt, concurrency (no lost entry), no-stamping (F6), and recovery-rehearsal. Capture the RED→GREEN paper trail in the PR.
tasks: Author fixtures from the REAL artifact schemas (P0-1): a backlog.json + a manifest.json ({schema_version, features:[{name, artifacts:{plan_json: bool,...}}]}) + plan.json set that exercises prd-only-stays, plan-committed-removed, ambiguous-match-stays (epic-name collision across features), malformed-shape-stays, and re-planned-epic-reorder-still-resolves (same epic name, different EPIC-N — P0-2).

Write evals asserting: validate_backlog rejects bad/duplicate-id entries; view renders correct status badges; manual remove deletes; eager prune and lazy sweep remove plan-committed entries idempotently.

Add a concurrency/lost-update eval (P1-1/security P1-a): assert a concurrent on-disk change between read and os.replace() is refused with BacklogInvalid (compare-before-replace) — no corruption AND no lost entry. This tests a detectable behavior, not a race the single-writer design forbids.
Add a write-side validation eval (security P1-b): a capture() that would produce a schema-invalid document raises BacklogInvalid and leaves backlog.json byte-unchanged (no .tmp promoted).
Add a no-stamping eval (F6): after promotion via /plan or /implement, assert plan.json and story records are byte-unchanged.

Add a recovery-rehearsal eval (security P1-c): after a simulated crash at the ordering seam (after .shield/backlog-removed.log append, before the destructive remove), assert the entry is recoverable by replaying the log — not just the clean wrong-removal case.

Name the CI entrypoint as a CONCRETE value (P1/DX): the actual workflow file + runner (e.g. .github/workflows/<file>.yml invoking the shield/evals/ runner), with the path-filter glob scoping 'backlog assets' (shield/{schema,scripts,skills/general/backlog}/**, shield/commands/backlog.md).
acceptance_criteria: An eval suite under shield/evals/ covers capture, view+status, manual remove, eager prune, lazy sweep, name-match-key, never-remove-on-doubt, re-plan epic-reorder, concurrency/lost-update detection, write-side refusal, no-stamping (F6), and ordering-seam recovery-rehearsal.
Fixtures use the real manifest.json/plan.json shapes (list-keyed features, boolean plan_json flag).
Evals are self-contained (no external API/LLM calls) and run via uv.
The PR body contains RED and GREEN sections demonstrating the behavior before and after.
The named CI workflow runs the eval on PRs touching the backlog-asset path glob.

stories.name: Version bump + command/skill docs
stories.description: Ship the feature: bump the Shield plugin version per CLAUDE.md (marketplace.json, and pyproject.toml for any Python package touched) in the same commit as the asset changes, and finalize the /backlog command + backlog SKILL.md docs (capture interface, reconciliation triggers, kill switch, manual remove, audit cadence).
tasks: Bump .claude-plugin/marketplace.json version for the Shield plugin entry.
Bump the backlog_store pyproject.toml — UNCONDITIONAL (P1-4): backlog_store is now a packaged importable module (EPIC-1-S2), so the version bump is not 'if packaged'.
Commit the shield/schemas/shield.schema.json 'backlog' object change (P0-3) in the same commit as the kill-switch asset changes.

Finalize shield/commands/backlog.md and shield/skills/general/backlog/SKILL.md: capture interface, three removal triggers, kill switch, name match key, manual remove, status badges, AND the wrong-removal recovery procedure (flip kill switch -> locate F9 log line -> replay .shield/backlog-removed.log).

Document the manual /backlog audit at a FIXED interval (monthly) with the concrete revisit trigger lifted from PRD §7: revisit scope if <70% of entries reach a terminal state within 30 days or >20% sit untouched >60 days.
Update Shield's CHANGELOG noting the backlog feature.
acceptance_criteria: The version is bumped in .claude-plugin/marketplace.json AND backlog_store's pyproject.toml, and the shield.schema.json backlog change is committed, all in the same commit as the asset changes.

/backlog command and backlog SKILL.md document capture, view+status, promote, manual remove, the three removal triggers, the kill switch, the wrong-removal recovery procedure, and the fixed monthly audit cadence with numeric revisit triggers.
Explicit DoD lines are present ('PR reviewed and merged', 'marketplace version published') so 'done' is unambiguous.
CHANGELOG mentions the backlog feature.

folded_findings: P0 gate-0d: TRD §2 paraphrased, not verbatim PRD §3
Backend P1-a: id contract (uuid4 + uniqueness) + duplicate-id AC (EPIC-1-S1)
Backend P2-a: single 'epic landed' predicate, story status not consulted (EPIC-3-S2)
SRE OP1: log every removal with rationale (EPIC-3-S2)
SRE P2: recovery-rehearsal eval + audit cadence (EPIC-4-S1/S2)

p0_applied: P0-1: reconcile/suggest_* typed against real manifest/plan shapes; plans=dict[slug->plan], path derived (EPIC-2-S2/EPIC-3-S2, TRD §11, LLDs)
P0-2: epic match by normalized NAME not positional EPIC-N id (EPIC-2-S2/EPIC-3-S2, M3 exit, TRD §5 F8, lld-reconciler)
P0-3: additive 'backlog' object added to shield/schemas/shield.schema.json so the kill switch validates (EPIC-3-S3/EPIC-4-S2)
p1_applied: P1-1: recovery mechanism resolved to .shield/backlog-removed.log; commit-before-prune is a non-goal (EPIC-3-S3, TRD §6 N4/§14)
P1 (backend/security): compare-before-replace lost-update detection (EPIC-1-S2, TRD §6 N1, lld-backlog-store)
P1-2: duplicate_entry_id enforced by validator not JSON Schema (EPIC-1-S1, TRD §5 F2)
P1-4: backlog_store packaged; unconditional pyproject bump (EPIC-1-S2/EPIC-4-S2)
